Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Swift Relocations rate increase notification.
Tool: search_emails (query related to "Swift" or "rate increase" or "surcharge" or "Greg Pallone", or similar)
Discovery: Greg Pallone's email (April 11) announcing 15% base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings, plus a $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment where Swift crews manage added compliance paperwork. The increase is driven by additional administrative load from retroactive placarding certificates, chain-of-custody logs, and hazmat declarations.
OE 2: Search emails for Heartland Movers rate increase / Q1 dispute correspondence.
Tool: search_emails (query related to "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "rate" or "Q1", or similar)
Discovery: Jake Loomis's formal notice (April 13) of a 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, explicitly tied to the unresolved Q1 invoice dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042). Jake warns that Heartland leadership may begin deprioritizing MoveOps jobs if the dispute is not settled. Also find Marcus Thorne's formal dispute email requesting a corrected invoice for $8,000 (5 verified moves only).
OE 3: Search Slack (#operations, channel C006) for vendor consolidation and Heartland Q1 dispute discussions.
Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (channel C006)
Discovery: Chloe's Heartland Q1 breakdown ($12,800 billed for 8 moves, only 5 verified = $8,000 legitimate, $4,800 overbilled for 3 moves: Lily Marchetti cancelled Feb 10, Danielle Osei cancelled Feb 13, Priya Venkatesh handled by Swift). Marcus's payment hold confirmation. Blessing's own post about Swift driver pushback on new paperwork. Elena's directive for a vendor strategy memo by Wednesday.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for the Heartland Q1 bill and payment hold status.
Tool: quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_get_bill
Discovery: bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold — $12,800 total amount, payment hold in place, 8 line items at $1,600 each, 3 annotated as DISPUTED (Line 6: Marchetti, Line 7: Osei, Line 8: Venkatesh). Bill is flagged as DISPUTED in QuickBooks.
OE 5: Search Airtable for Marcus Webb (Canopy Health) relocation record.
Tool: airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base appMoveOpsOps001, table Relocations)
Discovery: recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago — Marcus Webb, Canopy Health, Detroit MI → Chicago IL, Status: In Progress, Move dates April 10-12, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor. Special requirements: SPLIT-VENDOR arrangement — Heartland Movers for household goods, Swift Relocations for cold-chain lab equipment (3 cryogenic items: 2× Thermo CryoPlus 3800 at -20°C, 1× MVE SC 20/20 Dewar flask). Lab equipment goes to Canopy Health Chicago lab receiving dock, NOT the apartment.
OE 6: Search Airtable for Kevin Okafor relocation record.
Tool: airtable_search_records (base appMoveOpsOps001, table Relocations)
Discovery: recReloc00000000030 — Kevin Okafor, Canopy Health, Phoenix AZ → Portland OR, Status: In Progress, Move dates April 14-16, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor. Requirements: 2BR apartment (has partner), pet-friendly, home office space, $2,000 equipment stipend. No specific vendor assignment listed in the record.
OE 7: Search Airtable for Preethi Nair relocation record.
Tool: airtable_search_records (base appMoveOpsOps001, table Relocations)
Discovery: recReloc00000000036 — Preethi Nair, BrightLoop Analytics, Atlanta GA → Raleigh NC, Status: In Progress, Move dates April 21-23, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor. Requirements: Studio or 1BR, pet-friendly (has a cat), economy flight, $1,800 equipment stipend. No specific vendor assignment listed.
OE 8: Search Linear for the Heartland Q1 dispute tracking ticket.
Tool: linear_list_issues (query related to "Heartland" or "vendor invoice", or similar)
Discovery: issue_heartland_q1_recon — "Vendor invoice reconciliation — Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)", State: in_progress, Assigned to Chloe Vance, Priority P2. Describes $4,800 overbilling across 3 moves. Chloe's comment notes Marcus sent formal dispute, and critically that Jake Loomis has a history of not replying — Chloe's February cancellation emails for Marchetti and Osei were never acknowledged by Heartland. This non-responsiveness is key context for the prompt's ask about whether "something's been sitting without a response from Jake."
OE 9: Search Linear for Hana Kim's broader reconciliation findings.
Tool: linear_list_issues (query related to "Heartland" or "billing discrepancies" or "consolidate", or similar)
Discovery: linear_issue_6f33a369d77e — "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response", Assigned to Marcus Thorne, Priority P1. Hana widened the Heartland review on April 14 and found discrepancies in 3 of 5 verified Q1 Heartland moves: Kevin Tran had 2 days of storage billed without signed authorization, additional issues on other moves.
OE 10: Search emails or CRM for Lakeline Moving / Sandra Walsh coverage information.
Tool: search_emails (query related to "Lakeline" or "Sandra Walsh" or "backup vendor", or similar) OR crm_search_companies (name: "Lakeline")
Discovery: Sandra Walsh (Director of Operations, Lakeline Logistics) email to Chloe confirming Lakeline can cover Midwest and Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX) at roughly 10% below Heartland pricing. Cannot cover West Coast lanes, not set up for international, and cannot fully replace a national carrier. Not a viable full replacement for Heartland or Swift.
OE 11: Search CRM for Marcus Webb Canopy Health deal and engagement notes.
Tool: crm_search_deals (dealname related to "Canopy" or "Webb", or similar) OR crm_list_engagements
Discovery: deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 — $3,200 deal, stage: inprogress. Engagement eng_canopy_webb_splitvendor_001 documents the split-vendor arrangement: Heartland confirmed they cannot handle cold-chain, Chloe approved the split-vendor solution. Outstanding item: Swift cold-chain freight quote was pending (requested April 3, needed by April 5). The Canopy Q2 invoice (INV-2026-0401, $9,210) includes a $1,200 cold-chain surcharge for Marcus Webb, suggesting the arrangement was priced.
OE 12: Look up Chloe Vance's email address for the outgoing email.
Tool: contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe" or "Vance", or similar)
Discovery: chloe.vance@moveops.com
OE 13: Update Airtable relocation records for Blessing's moves where vendor situation or status needs correction.
Tool: airtable_update_records (base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01)
Action: Update records (e.g., recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago, recReloc00000000030, recReloc00000000036) with notes about vendor rate impact, vendor assignment status, or status corrections as appropriate.
OE 14: Create a Linear issue to track vendor rate increase impact on active operations.
Tool: linear_create_issue (team: team_operations)
Action: Create issue with title related to vendor rate increase impact on active relocations (or similar). Description should reference Heartland's 20% Q3 increase, Swift's 15% immediate increase + $500 DOT surcharge, and list affected moves or coordinators.
OE 15: Post a vendor status summary in #operations.
Tool: conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006)
Action: Post message summarizing the vendor rate situation, which moves/coordinators are affected, Lakeline backup viability, and current state of the Heartland dispute.
OE 16: Send email to Chloe Vance with Blessing's exposure assessment.
Tool: send_email
Action: From blessing.okafor@moveops.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com. Content covers: which of Blessing's moves are exposed to vendor rate changes (Marcus Webb split-vendor at highest risk, Kevin Okafor and Preethi Nair vendor assignments unclear), Lakeline's coverage limitations, what Blessing needs from Chloe to keep moves on track.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #2The trajectory shows a successful airtable_update_records call targeting base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, updating records recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago, recReloc00000000030 (Kevin Okafor), recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair), and recReloc00000002 (Marchetti) with vendor risk/status notes.
✓Run #3airtable_update_records was called twice successfully: once for recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago with vendor risk flag notes, and once as a batch for recReloc00000000030 (Kevin Okafor) and recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair) with vendor assignment status and rate impact notes.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows a successful airtable_update_records call on base appMoveOpsOps001/tblRelocations01, updating recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago, recReloc00000000030 (Kevin Okafor), recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair), and recReloc00000002 (Marchetti) with vendor rate impact notes and status corrections.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows a successful airtable_update_records call targeting records recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago, recReloc00000000030 (Kevin Okafor), and recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair) in base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01. All three were updated with vendor rate impact and assignment status notes. The result confirmed success.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows four successful airtable_update_records calls targeting recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago (vendor risk flags), recReloc00000000030 (Kevin Okafor, no vendor warning), recReloc00000000036 (Preethi Nair, Lakeline viability), and recReloc00000002 (Lily Marchetti corrected to Cancelled), all in base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01.

outcome

outcome
